Title: Schedule a virtual visit for a patient with valid provider and time slot
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; patient and tele-capable provider exist; provider has open slot
Test_Steps: 1. Open scheduling screen | 2. Search and select patient | 3. Select Visit Type = Telemedicine | 4. Select provider with open slot | 5. Pick date and time | 6. Click Schedule
Test_Data: Patient=Maya Synth (ID TST-1001); Provider=Dr. Ortega; VisitType=Telemedicine; Slot=05-Jul-2026 10:00
Expected_Result: Virtual visit is created with status Scheduled, a unique appointment ID is generated, and a join link is provisioned for both patient and provider

Title: Prevent double-booking the same provider into an overlapping virtual slot
Preconditions: Provider Dr. Ortega already has a Telemedicine visit at 10:00-10:30
Test_Steps: 1. Open scheduling screen | 2. Select another patient | 3. Choose Dr. Ortega | 4. Pick overlapping slot 10:15 | 5. Click Schedule
Expected_Result: System blocks the booking and shows a conflict error; no second appointment is created

Title: Schedule visit at the boundary of provider availability window (first and last bookable slot)
Preconditions: Provider availability is 09:00-17:00 with 30-min slots
Test_Steps: 1. Book the 09:00 slot | 2. Verify success | 3. Book the 16:30 slot | 4. Verify success | 5. Attempt to book 17:00
Expected_Result: 09:00 and 16:30 succeed; 17:00 is rejected as outside availability

Title: Schedule across time zones converts and stores appointment in correct local time
Preconditions: Scheduler in EST; patient profile time zone is PST
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule visit for patient at 10:00 patient-local | 2. Save | 3. Open appointment as provider in EST | 4. Verify displayed time
Expected_Result: Appointment stores UTC canonical time and displays 13:00 EST to provider and 10:00 PST to patient with no drift

Title: Generate unique secure join link with expiry on appointment creation
Preconditions: Virtual visit scheduled successfully
Test_Steps: 1. Open the appointment | 2. Retrieve join link | 3. Inspect URL token and expiry | 4. Decode token claims
Expected_Result: Join link contains a high-entropy single-use token, is HTTPS only, and carries an expiry timestamp not exceeding visit window plus grace period

Title: Reject expired join link attempt
Preconditions: Join link generated and its expiry time has passed
Test_Steps: 1. Wait until link expiry passes | 2. Open the join link in browser | 3. Observe response
Expected_Result: System denies access with an Expired Link message and does not load the video session

Title: Reuse of single-use join token is blocked after first successful join
Preconditions: Patient already joined once using single-use token
Test_Steps: 1. Join successfully with token | 2. End/leave session | 3. Attempt to reuse same token URL
Expected_Result: Second attempt with same single-use token is rejected; patient must request a fresh link

Title: Tampered join token signature is rejected
Preconditions: Valid join link issued for a visit
Test_Steps: 1. Capture valid join URL | 2. Modify a character in the token | 3. Open tampered URL
Expected_Result: Signature validation fails and access is denied with no session created and an audit log entry written

Title: Patient enters waiting room before provider joins and sees waiting status
Preconditions: Patient has valid join link; provider not yet joined
Test_Steps: 1. Patient opens join link | 2. Complete device check | 3. Enter waiting room | 4. Observe status
Expected_Result: Patient is placed in the waiting room with a Waiting for provider message; provider dashboard shows patient as Waiting

Title: Provider admits patient from waiting room into the session
Preconditions: Patient is in waiting room; provider is logged in
Test_Steps: 1. Provider opens waiting room queue | 2. Select patient | 3. Click Admit | 4. Observe session start
Expected_Result: Visit transitions Waiting -> InSession; both parties connect; appointment status updates to In Progress

Title: Waiting room queue ordering reflects arrival order across multiple patients
Preconditions: Three patients hold valid links for the same provider
Test_Steps: 1. Patient A joins | 2. Patient B joins | 3. Patient C joins | 4. Provider views queue
Expected_Result: Queue lists patients in arrival order A,B,C with accurate wait timers

Title: Start video and audio session with both streams active
Preconditions: Patient admitted; both devices have working camera and mic and granted permissions
Test_Steps: 1. Provider admits patient | 2. Both grant camera and mic | 3. Session establishes | 4. Verify two-way video and audio
Expected_Result: Bidirectional video and audio are established; both participants see and hear each other within acceptable latency

Title: Join with camera blocked but microphone allowed (audio-only degraded mode)
Preconditions: Patient device has mic permission granted, camera permission denied
Test_Steps: 1. Patient joins | 2. Allow mic, deny camera | 3. Enter session | 4. Observe media state
Expected_Result: Session proceeds in audio-only mode; UI clearly indicates camera off; provider is notified video is unavailable

Title: Media permission decision table across camera and mic states
Preconditions: Patient on supported browser with controllable permissions
Test_Steps: 1. Set camera/mic to each combination | 2. Join | 3. Record resulting mode | 4. Repeat for all four combinations
Expected_Result: AV when both allowed; audio-only when only mic allowed; session blocked with guidance when mic denied regardless of camera

Title: Automatic reconnection after transient network drop during session
Preconditions: Active video session in progress
Test_Steps: 1. Establish session | 2. Disable network for 10 seconds | 3. Re-enable network | 4. Observe reconnection
Expected_Result: Session shows Reconnecting then auto-restores media within the grace window without restarting the visit; status returns to In Progress

Title: Prolonged disconnection beyond grace period ends session gracefully
Preconditions: Active session; reconnection grace window is 60 seconds
Test_Steps: 1. Drop network for 90 seconds | 2. Observe session state at 60s boundary | 3. Restore network
Expected_Result: At 60s the session is marked Disconnected; both parties see a clear ended/dropped message and can rejoin via a fresh link

Title: Low bandwidth automatically degrades video quality without dropping audio
Preconditions: Active AV session on a throttled connection
Test_Steps: 1. Start AV session | 2. Throttle bandwidth to 256 kbps | 3. Observe adaptive behavior
Expected_Result: Video resolution downgrades adaptively while audio remains continuous and intelligible

Title: Capture patient e-consent for telehealth before session start
Preconditions: Patient in waiting room; jurisdiction requires telehealth consent
Test_Steps: 1. Present telehealth consent form | 2. Patient reads terms | 3. Patient checks agreement and signs | 4. Submit
Expected_Result: E-consent is recorded with timestamp, version, and patient identity; session is allowed to start only after consent captured

Title: Block session start when telehealth consent is declined or missing
Preconditions: Jurisdiction requires telehealth consent; patient declines
Test_Steps: 1. Present consent form | 2. Patient declines | 3. Attempt to start session
Expected_Result: Session start is blocked; provider sees a Consent Required notice; decline is logged with timestamp

Title: Persist e-consent as FHIR Consent resource with correct status and scope
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint enabled; telehealth consent just captured
Test_Steps: 1. Capture consent | 2. GET Consent resource from FHIR API | 3. Validate fields
Expected_Result: FHIR Consent resource returns with status=active, correct patient reference, scope, category coding, and dateTime

Title: Capture explicit recording consent from both parties before recording starts
Preconditions: Recording feature enabled; session active
Test_Steps: 1. Provider requests recording | 2. System prompts both parties | 3. Capture each response | 4. Evaluate combinations
Test_Data: Combos: both consent->record; either declines->no record
Expected_Result: Recording starts only when both parties consent; if either declines, recording does not start and the decision is logged

Title: Recording indicator is visible to all participants while recording is active
Preconditions: Both parties consented and recording started
Test_Steps: 1. Start recording | 2. Observe each participant UI | 3. Stop recording
Expected_Result: A persistent recording indicator is shown to every participant throughout, and disappears when recording stops

Title: Recorded media is stored encrypted and access is restricted to authorized roles
Preconditions: A recording exists for a completed visit
Test_Steps: 1. Locate stored recording | 2. Inspect storage encryption | 3. Attempt access as unauthorized role | 4. Attempt access as authorized role
Expected_Result: Recording is encrypted at rest; unauthorized role is denied; authorized access is granted and logged in the audit trail

Title: Provider writes and saves a clinical note during the active session
Preconditions: Provider in active session with patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open notes panel | 2. Enter clinical observations | 3. Save note | 4. Verify it attaches to the visit encounter
Expected_Result: Note is saved to the encounter for this visit with author, timestamp, and visit linkage; visible on patient chart

Title: Provider places a medication order during a virtual visit
Preconditions: Provider in session; patient has active chart; ordering privileges granted
Test_Steps: 1. Open orders panel | 2. Search drug | 3. Set dose, route, frequency | 4. Sign order
Expected_Result: Order is created against the telemedicine encounter, requires provider signature, and appears in the active orders list

Title: Drug interaction warning surfaces when ordering a contraindicated drug in-session
Preconditions: Patient has an active anticoagulant on the chart
Test_Steps: 1. Open orders | 2. Order an interacting NSAID | 3. Sign
Expected_Result: An interaction alert is displayed before signing; provider must acknowledge or cancel; override reason is captured if proceeding

Title: End session and require encounter documentation before closure
Preconditions: Active session with unsaved note draft
Test_Steps: 1. Provider clicks End Visit | 2. System prompts for required documentation | 3. Complete note | 4. Confirm end
Expected_Result: Session transitions InSession -> Completed only after required documentation is provided; appointment marked Completed

Title: Visit duration and participant join/leave times are recorded accurately
Preconditions: Completed telemedicine visit with known join and leave timestamps
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a visit | 2. Query session record in database | 3. Compare recorded times to observed
Expected_Result: Stored session record contains accurate start, end, duration, and per-participant join/leave timestamps

Title: Fallback to telephone visit when video cannot be established
Preconditions: Patient unable to establish video after repeated attempts
Test_Steps: 1. Patient fails video twice | 2. Provider selects Switch to Phone | 3. System provides callback flow | 4. Complete consult by phone
Expected_Result: Visit continues as a documented phone encounter; modality is recorded as Telephone; documentation requirements still enforced

Title: Modality change from video to phone is captured in the encounter audit trail
Preconditions: Visit started as video then switched to phone
Test_Steps: 1. Start video visit | 2. Switch to phone | 3. Complete visit | 4. Review audit log
Expected_Result: Audit trail records modality change with user, timestamp, and reason; encounter shows final modality Telephone

Title: Join virtual visit from mobile browser and desktop yields equivalent functionality
Preconditions: Valid join link; access from multiple device/OS/browser pairs
Test_Steps: 1. Join on iOS Safari | 2. Join on Android Chrome | 3. Join on Windows Edge | 4. Verify AV and controls on each
Expected_Result: Each device/browser pair establishes AV and exposes core controls (mute, camera, end) consistently

Title: Resume session on a second device after first device fails mid-visit
Preconditions: Active session on device A; valid means to rejoin
Test_Steps: 1. Join on device A | 2. Force-close device A app | 3. Rejoin on device B with link | 4. Observe continuity
Expected_Result: Patient rejoins on device B and reconnects to the same active visit; provider sees uninterrupted visit context

Title: Waiting room and session controls are operable via keyboard and screen reader
Preconditions: Session UI loaded; screen reader enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate controls with keyboard only | 2. Verify focus order and visible focus | 3. Verify ARIA labels announce mute/camera/end | 4. Check captions toggle
Expected_Result: All interactive controls are reachable and operable by keyboard with logical focus order and descriptive screen-reader labels meeting WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Live captions available during audio for hearing-impaired patients
Preconditions: Captioning feature enabled; active session
Test_Steps: 1. Start session | 2. Enable captions | 3. Speak test phrases | 4. Verify caption rendering
Expected_Result: Captions render in near real time with reasonable accuracy and can be toggled by the user

Title: Create telemedicine appointment via scheduling API returns valid resource
Preconditions: API client authenticated with scheduling scope
Test_Steps: 1. POST appointment with telemedicine type | 2. Capture response | 3. GET created appointment | 4. Validate fields
Expected_Result: API returns 201 with appointment ID, status Scheduled, and a join link reference; GET confirms persisted data

Title: Unauthorized API call to fetch another patient join link is rejected
Preconditions: API client authenticated as a user without rights to target patient
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate as User X | 2. Request join link for patient not assigned to User X | 3. Observe response
Expected_Result: API returns 403 Forbidden; no join link or PHI is disclosed; attempt is audit-logged

Title: Telemedicine encounter exposed as FHIR Encounter with virtual class code
Preconditions: Completed telemedicine encounter; FHIR API enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET Encounter resource | 2. Inspect class and type | 3. Validate participant and period
Expected_Result: Encounter returns with class indicating virtual/telehealth (e.g. VR), correct subject, participant, status=finished, and period

Title: ADT message reflects telemedicine visit registration with correct patient class
Preconditions: HL7 interface configured; telemedicine visit registered
Test_Steps: 1. Register telemedicine visit | 2. Capture outbound ADT^A04 | 3. Parse PV1 segment
Expected_Result: ADT message carries the correct patient class for outpatient/virtual visit and accurate visit identifiers in PV1

Title: ORM/ORU for in-session order propagates to downstream system
Preconditions: HL7 order interface configured; order placed in session
Test_Steps: 1. Place lab order in session | 2. Capture outbound ORM^O01 | 3. Validate OBR and ORC segments
Expected_Result: Order message is generated with correct ordering provider, order control code, and visit linkage

Title: Concurrent admit of same waiting-room patient by two provider sessions is handled safely
Preconditions: Patient in waiting room; two provider browser sessions open the queue
Test_Steps: 1. Both sessions click Admit nearly simultaneously | 2. Observe resulting state | 3. Query session rows
Expected_Result: Exactly one active session is created; the second admit is rejected or merged; no duplicate session rows persist

Title: Exploratory session on rapid join/leave/rejoin and control toggling
Preconditions: Active telemedicine session available for exploration
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly toggle mute and camera | 2. Leave and rejoin several times | 3. Switch networks | 4. Observe UI and state stability
Expected_Result: No crashes, stuck states, ghost participants, or stale recording indicators; UI state remains consistent and recoverable

Title: No-show handling when patient never joins within the visit window
Preconditions: Visit scheduled; patient never opens join link before window closes
Test_Steps: 1. Let visit window elapse with no patient join | 2. Observe appointment state | 3. Check provider notification
Expected_Result: Appointment auto-transitions to No-Show after the window; provider is notified; slot is freed per policy

Title: E-consent versioning requires re-consent when telehealth terms change
Preconditions: Patient previously consented to v1; terms updated to v2
Test_Steps: 1. Update consent template to v2 | 2. Patient starts new visit | 3. Observe consent prompt
Expected_Result: Patient is prompted to consent to v2 before session start; v1 record is retained and v2 captured separately

Title: Session media is end-to-end secured and not accessible to uninvited users via link guessing
Preconditions: Active session with known appointment ID pattern
Test_Steps: 1. Derive plausible adjacent session URLs | 2. Attempt to join as uninvited user | 3. Observe access control
Expected_Result: Uninvited user cannot join; access requires a valid token bound to identity; media transport is encrypted; attempts are logged

Title: Pairwise device, OS, browser, and network coverage for join reliability
Preconditions: Multiple test environments available
Test_Steps: 1. Execute pairwise matrix of device x OS x browser x network | 2. Join each combination | 3. Record AV success and control availability
Expected_Result: Each selected pairwise combination joins successfully with functional AV and controls; failures are categorized by factor
